# L,D,&C Co-Op

**Status:** Disputed

Freelancer cooperative providing tech packs, patterns, and sample making for 5 underwear styles.

### Styles

1. **G-String** (GST-025)
2. **Cotton Mid-Rise Thong** (MTB-025)
3. **Stretch Mesh Mid-Rise Thong** (MTB-025)
4. **High Waist Hipster** (HWH-025)
5. **High Cut Brief** (HCB-025)

### Key Terms

- 3 revisions included per design for tech packs/patterns
- Sample amendments generally not possible (physical work)
- Late fee: 2% per month on overdue invoices
- Delayed client feedback fee: 1% per week
- 7-day written notice required for termination
- Cancellation requires payment for completed work
- **Contract auto-terminates January 30, 2026**

## Issues & Disputes

### Issue #0: Early Warning - Samples Already Off Spec (Oct 2025)

**Reported:** October 31, 2025 (by Leslye)

Leslye noticed during initial tech pack work that the physical samples were measuring off from the DIY tech pack specs. She asked which measurements to use - the written specs or the actual sample measurements.

**Significance:** This early warning indicated potential spec/sample alignment issues that would recur throughout the project.

---

### Issue #1: G-String Spec Discrepancies (Dec 2025)

**Reported:** December 6, 2025

XS/S G-String sample did not match tech pack specs:
- Gusset width: 4.5" (should be 4")
- Back seam width: 2.25" (should be 1.75")
- Back waist width: 3.25" (should be 2.75")

**Vendor Response (Caroline):** Claimed this is "typical when transitioning a garment to a new team and factory" and that "most sample rooms go through two or three iterations."

**Vendor Response (Diane, Dec 8):** Took responsibility for timeline delays. Explained:
- She spent time trying to open factory pattern files (which turned out to be unusable - "factories hate sharing their work")
- This delayed Caroline who was left in a "bad position as the 'end of line' puzzle piece"
- They fell out of normal QC protocols because they were rushing
- Caroline prioritized sewing quality over documentation/measuring before shipment

**Diane's exact words:** *"Instead of rushing through the samples so that she could document and measure every piece, she wanted to ensure the sewing was done with care."*

**Our Position:** Samples should have been measured before shipping. XS/S was supposed to match the reference sample exactly.

**Resolution (Dec 12):** Diane confirmed all G-String pattern revisions were made and provided detailed spec alignment notes.

---

### Issue #2: Major Quality Failures Across All Styles (Jan 2026)

**Reported:** January 29, 2026

Samples received with extensive problems:

#### High Waist Hipster
- ❌ **Wrong fabric** - 100% pima cotton used instead of Supima/Modal per tech pack
- ❌ **Missing construction** - Elastic not tunneled in leg holes per tech pack
- ❌ **Wrong construction** - Has back seam when it shouldn't
- ❌ **Wrong specs** - Gusset width and side seam length incorrect

#### G-String
- ❌ Incorrect waistband specs
- ❌ POMs falling out of spec
- ❌ Specs don't match email revisions

#### High Cut Brief
- ❌ Tapering instructions from emails ignored
- ❌ Specs don't match email revisions

**Caroline's Acknowledgment:** Admitted fault for wrong fabric and missing leg elastic on High Cut Brief - was checking callouts page and graded spec page but not the BOM.

---

### Cancelled & Paused Work

Following the Jan 29 quality issues, the following work was cancelled or paused:

**CANCELLED:**
- High Waist Hipster / Heather Grey / L/XL
- High Waist Hipster / Teal / 1X/2X
- High Waist Hipster / Teal / XS/S
- Stretch Mesh Mid-Rise Thong / Lavender / L/XL

**PAUSED (pending meeting):**
- Cotton Mid-Rise Thong (all sizes)
- High Cut Brief Round #3 revisions
- G-String Round #3 revisions
- High Waist Hipster Round #2 revisions
- High Waist Hipster Round #1 remake

---

### Resolution Status

**Meeting scheduled:** January 30, 2026 at 11am EST to discuss issues.

**Contract note:** Contract auto-terminates on January 30, 2026 unless extended.

### Voicemails

See `documents/ldc-coop/voicemails/`:

#### 2026-01-14 - Diane Card (Pattern Maker)

**File:** `voicemail-3DC8D9FE-0734-4383-91D5-B67ED26760DE.m4a` (transcript: `.srt`)

**Summary:** Diane calls about the thong pattern. She can hit spec on everything except the leg opening. She explains this is because the spec is based on the finished garment. She cannot get the leg hole small enough to match spec while keeping the center front length, center back length, and waistband measurements. She requests approval to get the leg hole as small as possible while matching waist and length specs, with Caroline easing it into the elastic during sewing.

**Transcript:**
> Hi Gabriella, it's Diane Card. How are you? Happy New Year.
>
> I wanted to quickly call because I figured this would be easier than texting. But please feel free just to text back if you're busy, or we can follow up with an email once we decide.
>
> Working on the thong and getting that ready to send to Caroline. And I can hit spec on pretty much every single thing except for leg opening. And I think it's because the spec that's here is based on the finished garment.
>
> So I wanted to just make sure and confirm with you because up until now we've come to the conclusion and agreement that I'm just matching spec on everything that's there, just match spec because we obviously we have the issues that came through on those first two.
>
> However, this one I literally just cannot hit the leg hole if I keep the length or the center front length and center back length as well as the waistband. The leg hole is just I can't get it small enough to match spec.
>
> So I wanted to just basically just get approval from you to just get the leg hole to be as small as I can while matching the waist and length specs. And then just allowing for Caroline to ease that into the elastic whenever she is sewing.
>
> If you don't mind giving me a text back just saying confirmed and approved or please just match spec. I'm not sure how I would even match it, but I would probably have to change the waist or I don't know.
>
> Anyway, let me know what your thoughts are. And I hope you're having a good day. Bye.

### Text Messages (Diane Card)

Extracted from iMessage. Phone: +1 310-908-4265

#### 2026-01-15 - Thong Spec Follow-up

> **Diane:** Hi Gabriella! Here are my measurements from the samples!
>
> **Diane:** I'll follow these measurements and send to plot for Caroline. 🙂 I can be sure Leslye updates on her end and then we should be all good
>
> **Diane:** Sorry, missed the 3x/4x input. This is the one I'm following
>
> **Me:** What's the front seam gusset width vs the front gusset width
>
> **Diane:** Ft gusset is the top of the gusset on the inside of the panty. Seam gusset is where the front sews to the back

#### 2026-01-14 - Voicemail Follow-up (Leg Hole Issue)

> **Me:** Hi diane - I'm in class. What's up?
>
> **Diane:** NP! It's not a rush and I'm not stopped waiting. Just check voicemail at your earliest convenience and let me know your thoughts
>
> **Me:** Hi! How small is the leg hole / how much would you need to increase the leg holes? Yes, the specs reflect the POMs of the finished garment.
>
> **Diane:** I'll have to tell you tomorrow. Making dinner now. 😃
>
> **Diane:** I actually think it will be just fine and Caroline will hit the finished measurement with the elastic but I wanted to be sure it was ok with you since we discussed making the pattern hit spec
>
> **Me:** I don't know how it works exactly, all I care about is if the finished sample hits the specs 😂 so do what you need to do cuz I trust you
>
> **Me:** Okay! I looked at the specs and measured my copy of the sample and the gusset widths for XS/S and S/M should be 4" not 3.5". Leslie should have measured this on the sample but it doesn't look like she did.
>
> **Diane:** Yep! I think she just got confused because when I asked her about the specs she said other than the sticky notes everything was good as is so she did copy some of them
>
> **Diane:** Essentially making sure the leg matches spec after she attaches elastic

#### 2025-12-18 - Pattern Revision Direction

> **Diane:** Good morning!! Circling back this morning on yesterday's text.
>
> **Me:** Hi Diane! Sorry, I was off yesterday for my friend's wedding. I will get back to you in a few.
>
> **Me:** The patterns you have do not reflect the revisions that leslye recorded. So you can create the new patterns to match leslye specs.
>
> **Diane:** We will make sure that's sent over and marked as paid when she is back in office. Noted on the hipster pattern revisions, thank you for clarifying!
>
> **Diane:** I will proceed forward on the rest of the styles in this same way… if the pattern doesn't match spec… make it match spec!
>
> **Me:** Okay awesome! Thanks for your patience. I appreciate you! Lmk if anything else comes up.
>
> **Diane:** Will do and I appreciate you as well!

#### 2025-10-31 - Early Project (Setting Expectations)
